overall_verdict: Net positive, and unusually cheap. A pure-documentation workstream with zero engine risk: every iteration was behaviour-preserving (313 tests green throughout) and the value is real - the docs now tell one coherent, current, and honest story instead of a pre-build plan dressed as current fact. The standout is the adversarial README: leading with 'why you should NOT use lume', tagging every claim [E]/[U], and making the two biggest claims (value buys back its cost; works for anyone but the author) visibly unevidenced. ADOPTERS.md turns the 'zero external users' caveat into a living, falsifiable artifact rather than a line of prose. The honesty policy was applied to the work itself - the smoke-tested install is marked both [E] (mechanical) and [U] (never live), not glossed. Cost was low: no rejects this workstream, four small slices, mostly text. The one caveat: this refresh improves how honestly lume describes itself; it does not change the underlying unmeasured-value problem - it just stops hiding it.

stage_verdicts.saved: inventoried + classified every doc, found the MIT-without-LICENSE defect, and assembled the honest disqualifiers up front
stage_verdicts.net: high - the 'why not use it' material was the real product of discovery

stage_verdicts.saved: 8 forks resolved with leans matching the operator's stated requirements; clean 3-slice ordering (reconcile -> README -> graph/artifacts)
stage_verdicts.net: positive - no slice had to be re-cut, no rejects downstream

stage_verdicts.saved: docs/ no longer read as current claims; the unmeasured assumptions named honestly
the front door now leads with disqualifiers and evidences/flags every claim - the centrepiece deliverable
MIT claim backed; connected vault graph (link-checked, no orphans/dead links); ADOPTERS as living evidence

done_when.clause: Reader gets an accurate, consistent picture; each docs/ file current or marked historical
done_when.evidence: vision/scope banner'd historical with reconciliations; constraints split current vs historical log; questions reconciled per-assumption

done_when.clause: User-facing install instructions exist, are correct, easy to find
done_when.evidence: canonical install in root README; plugin/README links to it (no drifting duplicate); python3 + lume seed documented

done_when.clause: Repo opens as an Obsidian vault: one cross-linked graph rooted at README, no orphans
done_when.evidence: relative-markdown links throughout; scripted link-check across 8 docs found no dead links and every doc reachable

done_when.clause: README leads with why-not, every claim evidenced or flagged, humble tone
done_when.evidence: 'Why you should NOT use lume' is the first section; [E]/[U] tags + closing Status & evidence table; no superlatives

done_when.clause: README set and docs/ mutually consistent
done_when.evidence: no stale .lume/engine or 227-tests refs; layout/status claims align across README, plugin/README, docs/

done_when.clause: No engine code changed; tests stay green
done_when.evidence: 313 tests green at every handback; only docs + LICENSE + ADOPTERS touched

done_when.clause: ADOPTERS.md exists, documents how to register, records lume as sole adopter, in the graph, machine-readable
done_when.evidence: fixed-column table + add-via-PR + format comment; linked from README; seeded with the one honest row

carry_forwards: The unmeasured-value problem the README now states honestly is the real next step: a workstream that actually MEASURES whether lume's ceremony buys back its cost (reject rates, re-orientation time, time-saved). The docs now make the gap visible; they do not close it.

Still no live-Claude-Code-session verification of the plugin install (carried from 0008); the README flags it [U]. A real external adopter would close both this and the 'zero external users' caveat at once.
A future lume verb could read ADOPTERS.md to look up adopters and gather usage insights (deferred by design this workstream).
Optional: a CHANGELOG and the non-plugin install path (pyproject/zipapp) remain unbuilt; not required.

sections.heading: What worked
sections.body: Treating the README as an adversary forced genuine honesty: the exercise of tagging every claim [E]/[U] surfaced that lume's two headline claims are unevidenced, which is more useful than any feature doc. ADOPTERS.md is a small idea with leverage - it makes a caveat falsifiable. Doing reconciliation (E1) before the README (E2) meant the README could link to already-corrected docs.

sections.heading: What to watch
sections.body: A documentation refresh is the easiest place to quietly overclaim - to write the aspirational version. The [E]/[U] rule is the guardrail and should outlive this workstream: any future doc edit that adds an unevidenced claim without a [U] flag is a regression. The honest-but-unflattering framing only stays honest if it is maintained.

sections.heading: Scope discipline
sections.body: No engine code, no relitigating settled decisions, no marketing polish - the workstream removed overselling rather than adding it. The deferred items (measurement, live-session check, ADOPTERS-reading verb) were named as carry-forwards, not smuggled in.
